the_signal.headline: Agent execution failures now demand incident response automation
the_signal.body: n8n's incident response workflow pattern shows SOC teams are building AI-powered triage into their alert handling—combining RAG, threat intel, and historical context to reduce MTTR. This isn't optional tooling anymore; it's the reliability floor for any revenue-critical agent system. GTM engineers need to audit whether their production agents have equivalent failure detection and remediation loops.

tool_drop.name: LoRA Speedrun
tool_drop.what: Public wall-clock leaderboard tracking fine-tuning techniques and optimization trade-offs.
tool_drop.why: If you're shipping fine-tuned models in production (not just RAG), this leaderboard gives you real benchmarks for latency vs. accuracy—critical for SLA-driven GTM systems.

thread_of_day.title: Should I use Claude Code or n8n? Five questions to answer it.
thread_of_day.takeaway: The choice isn't binary—it's about whether you need observable, scalable workflows with compliance audit trails (n8n) or fast iteration with less ops overhead (Claude Code), and most production GTM systems need both in different layers.

quote.text: Agent identity, reliable execution, and intent are only half-way solved—after 75 expected capabilities from major platforms, core gaps remain in how agents persist state, recover from failures, and disambiguate intent at scale.

whats_moving.context: LoRA Speedrun leaderboard going public signals that fine-tuning latency is now competitive differentiation for production systems, not a nice-to-have.
n8n's incident response patterns show SOC teams are automating threat triage with RAG + historical context, raising the bar for what 'reliable agent' means in production.
n8n's compliance automation guide highlights that GTM engineers now need to choose between build-vs-buy with explicit infrastructure control requirements—no more one-size-fits-all platforms.

act_on_this: Audit your production agents for failure detection gaps: Do you have automated incident detection and context-enriched remediation (like the RAG + threat intel pattern n8n describes), or are you only alerting humans after failures compound?

numbers.stat: $500k vs. $25—exploit broker pricing gap for WordPress RCEs discovered via GPT vs. traditional methods
numbers.context: This wage arbitrage matters for GTM security: your revenue systems are now as exposed as your WordPress instances, and the incentive gradient for adversaries just got steeper.
